message:
fix(csrf): remove weak fallback, require cryptographic entropy

Changes CSRF token generation to panic if /dev/urandom is unavailable:

Before: Fell back to timestamp/counter/thread-id mix through FNV hash
After: Panics with clear error message if CSPRNG unavailable

Security rationale:
- CSRF tokens MUST be cryptographically unpredictable
- A predictable fallback defeats the purpose of CSRF protection
- Better to fail loudly than silently downgrade security
- Modern systems always have /dev/urandom available

Removes ~50 lines of generate_fallback() code that could never be
safely used. The panic message explains the requirement clearly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
